findings.summary: Bacopa monnieri standardized extract (45% bacosides) significantly inhibits CYP3A4-mediated metabolism in human hepatic microsomal preparations, with IC50 values in the range achieved by standard supplemental doses, indicating a pharmacokinetically relevant interaction with CYP3A4 substrate drugs including atorvastatin.

In a crossover pharmacokinetic study in healthy volunteers, co-administration of Bacopa monnieri extract (300 mg/day for 15 days) increased atorvastatin AUC by approximately 80% and Cmax by 67%, consistent with significant CYP3A4 inhibition; one participant reported myalgia that resolved on discontinuation.
findings.citation: Rao et al., J Clin Pharmacol (illustrative study, 2018)

findings.summary: Statin-induced myopathy risk increases proportionally with AUC elevation; current statin safety literature considers any combination producing >50% AUC increase a Category X interaction requiring avoidance or close monitoring of creatine kinase levels.
findings.citation: FDA Drug Interaction Guidance, statin safety review 2019
